---
title: "smooth_partial_effect"
generated: true
description: "Evaluate one smooth's partial effect on a covariate grid."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.gam`](../nitrix-stats-gam.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:smooth_partial_effect -->
```python
smooth_partial_effect(result: GAMResult, smooth_index: int, basis: Smooth, x: Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:smooth_partial_effect -->
Evaluate one smooth's partial effect on a covariate grid.

Computes the per-element fitted smooth $B(x)\,\gamma_k$ and its
pointwise standard error from the Bayesian covariance block (for a credible
band).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:smooth_partial_effect -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | — | — | A [`GAMResult`](../nitrix-stats-gam/GAMResult.qmd) from [`gam_fit`](../nitrix-stats-gam/gam_fit.qmd). |
| `smooth_index` | `int` | — | — | Index of the smooth term (into `result.col_slices`) to render. |
| `basis` | `Smooth` | — | — | The smooth basis used to build this term. For a [`TensorBasis`](../nitrix-stats-basis/TensorBasis.qmd) pass `x` as a tuple of matched per-margin grids (all length `g`) and the effect is the interaction surface along that path. For a [`REBasis`](../nitrix-stats-basis/REBasis.qmd) pass `x` as the integer level indices to read off, and the effect is the per-level random effect (the best linear unbiased predictor intercept, or the random-slope coefficient) at those levels. |
| `x` | `Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]` | — | — | `(g,)` covariate grid, or a tuple of per-margin grids for a tensor smooth, at which to evaluate the effect. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]`. The fitted partial effect at each grid point, per element. The pointwise standard error of the effect, per element.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
